intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2 (Mexican Mint) are not visible as distinct, healthy specimens. P3 (Pothos) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct entities. The scene is dominated by soil, debris, and a white rabbit figurine.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: Pothos foliage is absent; only the white rabbit anchor remains.

plant_audit.P1: Bare soil observed in yellow pot.
plant_audit.P2: Bare soil observed in black pot.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; no Pothos foliage detected.
plant_audit.P4: Bare soil observed in black pot.

vision_report.biome_observations: Soil appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or lacking drainage. There is significant debris (white fragments, possibly eggshells or perlite) on the surface of the black pot. The desk surface is dark and featureless.
vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to T-2, the pots remain static with no visible plant growth. From T-1 to CURRENT, the camera feed has suffered a total loss of illumination, rendering the biome invisible.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total failure. No living plant tissue is visible in any of the pots. The lack of light in the final two frames suggests either a power failure to the LED system or a complete cessation of monitoring.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white fragments in the soil is a persistent intervention. The total black-out in the final two images is a critical system anomaly.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of all botanical specimens. The pots contain only soil and debris. The white rabbit anchor is the only non-botanical element remaining. The final two images show a complete loss of visual data, indicating the biome is no longer being illuminated or observed correctly.
